findings.description: Header and footer layout is not preserved.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the header contains a right-aligned section with names and titles, and the footer is centered at the bottom. In the HTML, these elements are all left-aligned and stacked vertically underneath the logo at the top of the page.
The 'NC' logo in the PDF has a white/transparent background, but it renders with a solid black background in the HTML.

findings.description: Missing underline formatting on list item titles.
findings.evidence: The introductory terms for each bullet point (e.g., 'Agriculture', 'Air Quality') are both bold and underlined in the PDF, but the HTML only retains the bold formatting.

findings.description: Missing underline formatting on list item titles.
findings.evidence: The introductory terms for each bullet point (e.g., 'Beneficial Reuse', 'Costs') are both bold and underlined in the PDF, but the HTML only retains the bold formatting.

findings.description: Missing underline formatting on list item titles.
findings.evidence: The introductory terms for each bullet point (e.g., 'Health Issues', 'Private Well Issues') are both bold and underlined in the PDF, but the HTML only retains the bold formatting.

findings.description: A single bullet point sentence is split into two separate list items across a page break boundary.
findings.evidence:
In the PDF, the sentence 'One comment suggested that capping the ash...' spans across pages 4 and 5 within a single bullet point. In the HTML, this is split across two list items: '
  • ... around the Lee facility. One
    • ' at the end of page 4, and '
  • comment suggested that capping the ash...
    • ' at the start of page 5.
